_doc: Controlled-but-expandable vocabulary for an asset's `kind` (genre). An ASSET is anything the owner authors (alone or with Claude); a SOURCE is others' work. `format` is how it renders (system-level, closed); `kind` is what it is (human-level, open). Adding a new kind is DELIBERATE: it must not duplicate/overlap an existing kind here, nor shadow a node knowledge_type (concept, technique, result, framework, problem, principle, source, hypothesis, bridge-note, research-cluster, discovery-stub). When introducing a kind, add it here with a one-line definition. Audit drift with: tools/audit_asset_kinds.py.

formats.interactive: Rendered as a self-contained HTML artifact (a `viz:` file under wiki/assets/viz/).
formats.writing: Rendered as prose (the node's markdown body) in the reader.

explainer.definition: Interactive HTML teaching artifact; opens full-screen in the reader.

position-paper.definition: Long-form argued prose advancing a thesis or strategy.
